title: You Walk, We Authenticate: Lightweight Seamless Authentication Based on Gait in Wearable IoT Systems

method.input_representation: Feature matrix consisting of 37 features (24 statistical and 13 behavioral/mechanical features including pitch, roll, yaw, and force)
method.training_strategy: 70% training set and 30% testing set via random shuffling

evaluation_protocol: Multi-class classification (identifying the specific user among 51 classes)

reported_limitations: temporarily abrupt or inconsistent physical movement
impact of carrying heavy weights or injuries on arm swing
performance degradation as number of users increases

usability.usability_issues: Continuous authentication is not desirable due to limited battery life of IoT devices

key_contribution: Proposed LiSA-G framework that uses both statistical and human-action-related (mechanical) features for gait authentication
Eliminated gait cycle detection to reduce authentication time and data volume
Demonstrated high accuracy using only one sampling period of sensor data
main_strength: Lightweight and efficient for wearable devices
Implicit authentication with no user interaction
main_weakness: Lack of evaluation against impersonation/spoofing attacks
insights.notes_for_review: The paper emphasizes that adding physical/mechanical traits (pitch, roll, yaw) significantly helps in distinguishing users compared to using only statistical features.
